definition: A cross-device presenter command vocabulary for a deck (or any sequenced surface): a clicker channel — `next`, `previous`, `goto(address)`, `blank`/`resume`, `start`/`end` — paired with a follow-the-presenter MULTIPLEX mode where one presenter device drives the current slide-addressing position and N follower devices mirror it live. The command set is transport-agnostic: it rides the Transport Negotiation protocol's CustomTransportProvider channel (BroadcastChannel same-origin, WebSocket/WebRTC cross-device) rather than defining its own wire.
usage: Homed in webrealtime; composes Transport Negotiation (the live channel) + slide-addressing (the position each command moves to) + the Advanceable-Sequence intent (what next/previous mean). Multiplex follow mode is presence over the same channel: the presenter publishes its slide-addressing position on each command, followers subscribe and jump (instant-jump restore, no transition storm). Deferred / lower priority (device-specific) — this entry fixes the contract vocabulary so the deferred build has a stable target; the runtime transport impl is FUI/plateau, not WE. Distinct from the second-window presenter sync (#1184): that is same-device cross-window, this is cross-device.
